title: Asset or Liability?
scenario: A ₹40,000 certification course could boost your skills and future salary. The same money could buy a fancier phone. Which is the better 'investment'?

choices.label: Buy the upgraded phone
choices.lesson: A phone on EMI or cash is a lifestyle liability — it loses value and adds running costs. It rarely earns anything back.

choices.label: Pay for the upskilling course
choices.lesson: Spending on skills can be a career asset: it may raise your future income, unlike a depreciating gadget. Assets put money in your pocket; liabilities take it out.
